name: Chavacano (Caviteño / Chabacano de Cavite)

ancestors.notes: Spanish is the lexifier; written with the Spanish alphabet.
Tagalog substrate (Manila Bay area): phonology and grammar.

notes: Caviteño Chabacano (Chabacano de Cavite), a Manila-Bay Spanish-lexifier creole of Cavite City, Luzon, Philippines — moribund, distinct from Zamboangueño (cbk-zam). STUB: no variety-specific grapheme map is claimed yet. In practice it uses the same Spanish-alphabet orthography and shares the core Chabacano phonology of cbk-zam (seseo, yeísmo, ⟨j g⟩ before front vowels = [h], ⟨v⟩ = [b]); the parent is set to cbk-zam so it inherits that system pending a dedicated Manila-Bay description. Documented differences from Zamboangueño (e.g. TMA particles ta/ya/di, Tagalog rather than Visayan substrate) are described in Sippola's APiCS survey but not yet encoded here.

sources.author: Sippola, Eeva
sources.title: Cavite Chabacano structure dataset (APiCS Survey chapter 46)
sources.publisher: Atlas of Pidgin and Creole Language Structures Online (APiCS), eds. Michaelis, Maurer, Haspelmath & Huber
sources.pages: Manila-Bay Chabacano phonology and morphosyntax; Tagalog substrate; TMA particles distinct from Zamboangueño.
sources.notes: Primary descriptive source for the Cavite variety; grapheme map to be derived from it.
